title: Casearia dolichanthera
intro: Casearia dolichanthera is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the "heartbreak tree" due to its traditional use in treating emotional and mental distress. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Casearia dolichanthera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Casearia dolichanthera is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of capsules and tablets to support mental health and well-being.
taxonomy: Casearia dolichanthera is classified within the plant family Salicaceae, commonly known as the willow family. This plant family comprises over 200 genera, including willows, poplars, and cottonwoods. The genus Casearia, to which C. dolichanthera belongs, is native to the tropical regions of the Americas, Africa, and Asia. It is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their opposite, simple leaves and small, white or greenish flowers. The classification of C. dolichanthera reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Salicaceae family.
